thag18.1:1.1: One shouldn’t go about surrounded, revered by a company:
thag18.1:1.2:
One gets distracted;
concentration is hard to gain.
thag18.1:1.3: Fellowship with many people is painful.
thag18.1:1.4: Seeing this, one shouldn’t approve of a company.
thag18.1:2.2:
one gets distracted;
concentration is hard to gain.
thag18.1:2.4: whoever misses the goal that brings bliss.
thag18.1:3.3: a subtle arrow, hard to extract.
thag18.1:3.4: Offerings are hard for a worthless man to let go. * * *
thag18.1:4.1: Coming down from my dwelling place,
thag18.1:4.2: I entered the city for alms,
thag18.1:4.3: stood courteously next to a leper eating his meal.
thag18.1:5.2: tossed me a morsel of food,
thag18.1:5.3: and as the morsel was dropping,
thag18.1:5.4: a finger fell off right there.
thag18.1:6.2: I ate that morsel of food,
thag18.1:6.3: and neither while eating it, nor having eaten,
thag18.1:7.1:
Whoever has mastered left-over scraps for food, smelly urine for medicine,
the foot of a tree for a dwelling, cast-off rags for robes:
He is a man of the four directions. * * *
thag18.1:9.3: Kassapa does jhāna with no clinging,
thag18.1:10.3: Kassapa does jhāna with no clinging,
thag18.1:11.3: Kassapa does jhāna with no clinging,
thag18.1:13.1: The color of blue-dark clouds, glistening,
thag18.1:13.2: cooled with the waters of clear-flowing streams
thag18.1:13.3:
covered with ladybugs:
Those rocky crags refresh me.
thag18.1:14.1: Like the peaks of blue-dark clouds,
thag18.1:15.1: Their lovely surfaces wet with rain,
thag18.1:16.1: This is enough for me— desiring to do jhāna,
thag18.1:16.3: enough for me— desiring the goal,
thag18.1:17.3: enough for me— desiring my duty,
thag18.1:18.2: like the sky covered over with clouds;
thag18.1:18.3: filled with flocks of various birds:
thag18.1:19.3: filled with flocks of various birds:
thag18.1:20.4:
Those rocky crags refresh me.
There is no such pleasure for me
thag18.1:21.1: in the music of a five-piece band
thag18.1:21.3: as there is when my mind is at one,
thag18.1:22.1: One shouldn’t do lots of work,
thag18.1:22.2: should avoid people, shouldn’t busy oneself.
thag18.1:22.4: whoever misses the goal that brings bliss.
thag18.1:23.1: One shouldn’t do lots of work,
thag18.1:23.2: should avoid what doesn’t lead to the goal.
thag18.1:25.1: Not better, he thinks himself better,
thag18.1:25.4:
the stiff-necked man.
But whoever isn’t stirred by the modes of
thag18.1:27.1: one who’s discerning, who acts as he says,
thag18.1:27.3: committed to tranquility of awareness, he
thag18.1:27.4: is the one the wise would praise.
thag18.1:28.2: for his fellows in the holy life,
thag18.1:28.3: is as far from true Dhamma
thag18.1:28.4: as the earth from the sky.
thag18.1:29.1: But those whose sense of shame & compunction
thag18.1:29.3: They have flourished in the holy life.
thag18.1:29.4: For them there’s no further becoming.
thag18.1:30.2: even though clad in a robe of cast-off rags,
thag18.1:30.3: like a monkey in a lion’s skin,
thag18.1:31.1: But a monk not conceited or vain,
thag18.1:31.3: his faculties restrained, shines because of his robe of cast-off rags,
thag18.1:31.4: like a lion in the cleft of a mountain. * * *
thag18.1:32.4: all of Brahmā’s retinue, stand with their hands over their hearts, paying homage to Sāriputta,
thag18.1:34.2: Homage to you, O superlative man—
thag18.1:34.3: of whom we have no direct knowledge even of that
thag18.1:34.4: in dependence on which you do jhāna.
thag18.1:35.3: of which we have no direct knowledge,
thag18.1:35.4:
though we have come as hair-splitting archers.’
Seeing Sāriputta, a man worthy of worship,
thag18.1:37.1: As far as this buddha-field extends
thag18.1:37.3: I’m the one outstanding in ascetic qualities.
thag18.1:37.4: There’s no one else like me.
thag18.1:38.1: The Teacher has been served by me;
thag18.1:39.5: by water, inclining to renunciation, detached
thag18.1:39.6: from the three planes of becoming.
thag18.1:40.1: He, the great sage, has the establishings of mindfulness as his neck,
thag18.1:40.3:
discernment as head.
Having great knowledge,
